mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the wrong direction.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rates reflect tightening labor markets and robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >65% of months where UNRATE declines, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in real sales of existing homes exceeding 0.5 percent signals accelerating consumer demand and housing market momentum, triggering positive S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield negative 5-day returns in >70% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when RSAFS growth falls below the 0.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index drops below 98.0, it indicates a contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, which forecasts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where INDPRO crosses below 98.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising federal funds rates and falling unemployment creates a policy tightening regime that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns, or marginal effects reverse when either series moves independently.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with accommodative federal funds rates signals real negative interest rates, which boosts nominal corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive returns fail to materialize when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0 while FEDFUNDS remains low, or the interaction effect yields a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 150,000 per month confirms a robust labor expansion regime, which immediately translates to positive S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >65% of triggered months, or predictive accuracy drops to zero when payrolls growth falls below the 150,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts below 850,000 units signal a cooling construction sector and weakening aggregate demand, which forecasts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during a housing downturn regime.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in >70% of months where HOUST falls below 850.0, or the housing signal loses explanatory power when GDPC1 growth remains above 3.0 percent.
